Topic 1: Collapsing ceasefire (Iran)

Topic 2: Tariffs destroyed

[214:24]the ones from Liberation Day, the ones that were being used, effectively for diplomacy

[214:29]against Canada, Europe, China, other countries.

[214:33]And so all the tariffs were rescinded and the Trump administration has to give a refund

[214:55]every country that had tariffs under that emergency provision.

[265:40]The Iran war went on from February 28th until four weeks ago.

[265:45]You might remember when the original ceasefire was announced.

[265:50]And this followed all of those deadlines and ultimatums.

[265:54]There's been a few phases in the war.

[265:57]We came in really heavy with Israel in our surprise attack on Iran, purpose of which

[266:03]was to topple their government, came in with everything we had, killed their supreme leader.

[266:08]And the idea was the government would collapse, regime change would happen organically from

[266:14]the people, and that would be the end of it.

[266:17]We thought it would be like Venezuela, but the days went on, weeks went on, and we realized

[266:24]The Iranian regime chose a new supreme leader.

[266:28]It seems that their military was decentralized and resilient enough that they were still

[266:33]capable of carrying out drone and missile attacks.

[266:36]And critically, Iran did move and was able to close the Strait of Hormuz.

[266:43]So, these were some of the things that took place that changed the nature of the war.

[266:47]We thought we would topple the government.

[266:49]We wouldn't have to worry about the straight.

[266:52]We thought we could topple the government, and that would be the end of it.

[266:56]But because Iran's regime was durable, they were able to still carry out attacks.

[267:03]And that gave them the capability to indefinitely close the straight.

[267:27]the country were still able to launch drones and missiles.

[267:35]And so they closed the straight,

[267:37]they were able to continuously keep the straight closed.

[267:39]And then that put pressure on the United States

[267:43]because of the global price of energy.

[267:45]And at that point, it became a war of attrition.

[267:49]And we've talked a lot about this.

[267:51]The United States thought that if we just kept up

[267:54]the military pressure,

[267:55]that would eventually get Iran to fold,

[267:58]even though we were under economic pressure.

[268:00]But as the weeks went by,

[268:04]There was nothing that we could do militarily, only using air and sea power that would prevent

[268:10]Iran from keeping the strait closed.

[268:13]So we're under economic pressure because of the exploding price of energy and other key

[268:18]commodities.

[268:20]And our military pressure on Iran seemed to be yielding increasingly minimal results.

[268:27]There was no return on investment there.

[268:31]We were hitting them harder and dropping more bombs

[268:33]and doing more things.

[268:34]And it did not affect their ability

[268:36]to launch drones and missiles

[268:38]that were preventing commercial ships

[268:40]from getting through the straight.

[268:42]So then it became very obvious that Trump was just gonna have to quit and end the war.

[268:47]And he was looking for ways out, looking for ways that we could increase the pressure on

[268:51]Iran.

[268:52]We talked about all those different options.

[268:54]They talked about invading the islands.

[268:56]They talked about bombing civilian infrastructure.

[268:59]They talked about paratroopers landing in the Iranian mainland.

[269:03]All sorts of things.

[269:07]and said, well, maybe we'll do all of it. Maybe we'll invade the islands and invade the mainland

[269:12]and bomb the infrastructure. And finally, when the bluffing didn't work, eventually Trump just

[269:18]sued for a ceasefire. And that's where we were four weeks ago. So you could say that those were

[269:24]two distinct periods. You had that first phase of the war where we were seeking a speedy regime

[269:35]quickly turned into a war of attrition. And we tried to win that again with overwhelming

[270:03]process to find a comprehensive peace. Well, notably Iran did neither of those things.

[270:14]initially. And throughout that two week period, there was only one meeting in Pakistan and
